examples.title: Polymorphism with Abstract Base Class
examples.description: Demonstrates runtime polymorphism using ABC and method overriding
examples.explanation: Shape is an ABC with abstract method area(). Circle and Rectangle override area() with their own implementations. When we iterate over shapes list and call area(), Python determines which method to call at runtime based on actual object type - this is runtime polymorphism.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of shapes

examples.title: Operator Overloading for Complex Numbers
examples.description: Demonstrates operator overloading using dunder methods
examples.explanation: __add__ and __sub__ are dunder methods that define behavior for + and - operators. When c1 + c2 executes, Python internally calls c1.__add__(c2). This allows custom classes to use familiar operator syntax.

examples.title: Duck Typing Polymorphism
examples.description: Demonstrates polymorphism without inheritance using duck typing
examples.explanation: draw_shape() doesn't check if argument is a specific type. It just calls draw() method. If object has draw() method, it works. This is duck typing - 'if it has draw(), treat it as drawable'. No inheritance hierarchy needed.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of shapes

examples.title: Simulating Method Overloading
examples.description: Demonstrates how Python simulates method overloading using default arguments
examples.explanation: Python doesn't support traditional method overloading. We simulate it using: 1) Default parameter values (b=0, c=0), 2) Variable arguments (*args). The last defined method with same name handles all calls.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of arguments
